name: cloze_prose_intermediate
description: Intermediate expression mining for prose/article style text.
system_prompt: You are a language-learning content editor. Output a JSON array only. Do not output explanations or code fences.

user_prompt_template.en: source_lang={source_lang} target_lang={target_lang} document_title={document_title} source_url={source_url} learning_mode={learning_mode} difficulty={difficulty} cloze_max_sentences={cloze_max_sentences} cloze_min_chars={cloze_min_chars} cloze_max_per_chunk={cloze_max_per_chunk} You will receive one or more chunks in this format: chunk_id=chunk_0001 chunk_text=\n Return a JSON array. Each item MUST include: { "chunk_id": "matching chunk id", "text": "cloze sentence(s)", "original": "plain text without cloze markers/html", "target_phrases": ["phrase 1", "phrase 2"], "note_hint": "optional hint" } Optional debug fields are allowed: - "selection_reason": short reason why this candidate is selected - "phrase_types": array of labels - "learning_value_score": numeric score - "expression_transfer": short transfer-oriented hint Global requirements: 1) text must contain at least one cloze: {{cN::PHRASE}}(hint). 2) cN index increases by appearance order in each text. 3) original must not contain cloze markers or html. 4) use ... for emphasis, do NOT use markdown **. 5) omit candidates shorter than cloze_min_chars or exceeding cloze_max_sentences. 6) if no suitable candidate exists, return an empty array. Material profile: prose_article. Intermediate rubric: - Prefer idiomatic collocations and sentence-level expression units. - Usually 1-2 clozes per candidate. - Keep candidates reusable across topics. - Avoid trivial single-verb targets. - Include expression_transfer when reusable function is clear; keep it <=120 chars. chunk_text: {chunk_text}
